# HillnoteWiki

> Convert your Hillnote workspace into a beautiful documentation website

Welcome to Hillnote's documentation. This workspace contains comprehensive guides and references for using Hillnote, an AI-powered note-taking application with built-in LLM support.

## Core Documentation

- [Start Here ](http://localhost:3000/doc/start-here): Hillnote is a local AI note-taking app with built-in LLM support, guiding users through workspace creation, AI tool connections, and file syncing.
- [About the Editor](http://localhost:3000/doc/about-the-editor): Hillnote editor overview: Features include markdown editing, scratch spaces, sidebar navigation, formatting tools, document linking, and source mode for advanced users.
- [Buglist](http://localhost:3000/doc/buglist): Have a bug to report?
You can write to us at hello@hillnote.com
- [Connect](http://localhost:3000/doc/connect): Hillnote connections
- [Connecting to Hillnote](http://localhost:3000/doc/connecting-to-hillnote): Hillnote facilitates seamless integration of local and premium AI models with document commenting and a pay-as-you-go credit system.
- [Dear MCP and NPM](http://localhost:3000/doc/dear-mcp-and-npm): Hillnotes MCP server facilitates AI tool connections for seamless note management and document interaction, prioritizing local data privacy.
- [Get in touch](http://localhost:3000/doc/get-in-touch): Hillnote encourages user feedback and contributions via email, promoting AIs role in future development.
- [Juuuuuuust the right Tool](http://localhost:3000/doc/juuuuuuust-the-right-tool): Hillnote tools are customizable HTML programs created via AI assistants to automate tasks and enhance workflow within the Hillnote workspace.
- [Make it yours](http://localhost:3000/doc/make-it-yours): Hillnote utilizes local AI models (LLMs) through Ollama, allowing users to experiment with models like Gemma and Llama directly on their computers. Users can configure prompts, add context from documents, and customize settings for optimal AI performance, leveraging GGUF formats for efficient local execution.
- [Roadmap ](http://localhost:3000/doc/roadmap): Product Roadmap
- [The Filesystem](http://localhost:3000/doc/the-filesystem): Hillnote facilitates AI agent workflows by organizing local files with workspaces, documents, and a registry for context and navigation.
- [Using AI in Hillnote](http://localhost:3000/doc/using-ai-in-hillnote): Hillnote empowers users to utilize AI models for document editing, content generation, and offline assistance via a library interface with tools like spellcheck, prompts, and deep thinking mode.

## Connect

- [Connect with ChatGPT](http://localhost:3000/doc/connect/aitools/connect-with-chatgpt): My favourite way to work with ChatGPT is to
ChatGPT only allows for custom extensions for ChatGPT Pro or enterprise users
- [Connect with Claude ](http://localhost:3000/doc/connect/aitools/connect-with-claude): My favourite way to work with Claude is to
- [Connect with Perplexity](http://localhost:3000/doc/connect/aitools/connect-with-perplexity): My favourite way to work with Perplexity is to
Perplexity only allows for connectors in the Mac App
- [Use with Grok, Gemini, Deepseek and more](http://localhost:3000/doc/connect/aitools/use-with-grok-gemini-deepseek-and-more): Grok, Deepseek Projects &amp; Gemini Gems
- [Hillnote NPM](http://localhost:3000/doc/connect/dev/hillnote-npm): @hillnote/wiki
Turn your Hillnote workspace into beautiful documentation for your Next.js projects. The @hillnote/wiki NPM package provides ready-to-use compone
- [Pair with Cursor](http://localhost:3000/doc/connect/pair-with/pair-with-cursor): My favourite way to work with Cursor is to
- [Pair with Github CoPilot](http://localhost:3000/doc/connect/pair-with/pair-with-github-copilot): My favourite way to work with Github CoPilot is to
- [GDrive](http://localhost:3000/doc/connect/sync-and-collaborate/gdrive): My favourite way to work with Google drive is to

## API Access

For programmatic access to documentation:

- REST API: http://localhost:3000/api/docs - Query documentation content
- Search API: http://localhost:3000/api/search?q=YOUR_SEARCH_TERM - Full-text search across all documents
  - Syntax: Use ?q= parameter, URL encode spaces as %20
  - Example: http://localhost:3000/api/search?q=connect%20with%20claude

## Additional Resources
- Main Site: http://localhost:3000
- Documentation Index: http://localhost:3000/doc
- Sitemap: http://localhost:3000/sitemap.xml
